verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/BiP is a canonical ER-lumen chaperone with a KDEL retention signal, resident intracellularly at baseline. However, it undergoes well-documented surface translocation in cancer cells and under cellular stress (ER stress, hypoxia, heat shock). Ecto-BiP is recognized as an immunogenic surface antigen on tumor cells and is the target of cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs (antibodies, CAR-T). This cell-state-induced ecto-presentation qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
